cronBrief:
☀️ **SNOWLINE MORNING BRIEF** - Thursday, Mar 26

⚠️ **AVY DANGER:**

**COLORADO:**
• Front Range: ⚪ No Rating
• Vail & Summit County: 🟠 Considerable
• Sawatch: 🟡 Moderate
• Aspen: 🟡 Moderate
• Gunnison: 🟡 Moderate
• Grand Mesa: 🟢 Low
• North San Juan: 🟡 Moderate
• South San Juan: 🟡 Moderate

**UTAH:**
• Salt Lake: 🟠 Considerable
• Ogden: 🟠 Considerable
• Provo: 🟡 Moderate
• Uintas: 🟡 Moderate
• Skyline: ⚪ No Rating
• Logan: 🟠 Considerable
• Moab: 🟡 Moderate
• Abajos: ⚪ No Rating

**CALIFORNIA:**
• Tahoe: 🟡 Moderate
• Eastern Sierra: 🟡 Moderate
• Shasta: 🟢 Low

🚨 **SPECIAL ADVISORIES:**
None active

⚠️ **AVY PROBLEMS TO WATCH:**
• **Wet Loose & Wet Slab** - Primary concern across ALL regions. Unseasonably hot temps = short or zero refreeze windows. If you sink boot-top deep in wet snow, get off steep slopes.
• **Persistent Slab** (CO/UT) - Still lurking on N/NE aspects at upper elevations. Vail/Summit and San Juans have active Dry January Layer issues.
• CAIC: "Avalanches being reported daily. Travel early if at all."
• UAC: Conditions "unprecedented in our lifetimes"

🌨️ **STORM WATCH:**
**Colorado:** Light system grazing mountains Thu night → Friday. Expecting <1" above 10k'. Cooling into Friday with highs dropping ~10°F. Slight chance showers Sat PM.
**Utah/California:** Dry through weekend. Warm pattern continues.

📢 **SOCIAL WORTHY:**
• 🔥 UAC's "unprecedented conditions" quote - great for engagement
• Week of wet avy activity - educational wet slide content opportunity
• Cooling trend Friday = brief window of corn conditions Sat AM
• Classic late March wet slide cycle storyline
